org.jlab.epsci.ersap.std.services

Class AbstractStreamSourceService<TwoStreamSource>

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.jlab.epsci.ersap.std.services.AbstractService
      • org.jlab.epsci.ersap.std.services.AbstractStreamSourceService<TwoStreamSource>
  • Type Parameters:
    TwoStreamSource - Two stream aggregator and decoder object
    All Implemented Interfaces:
    Engine

    public abstract class AbstractStreamSourceService<TwoStreamSource>
extends AbstractService
    This abstract class assumes that the TwoStreamSource is a back-end server that listens JLAB VTP streams.

    • Field Summary

      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      protected TwoStreamSource streamSource
      The TwoStreamSource object.

      Closes the stream source.

      Configures the engine with the given input data.

      Executes the engine with the given input data.

      Gets the ERSAP engine data-type for the type of the stream.

      Gets the set of input data types supported by the engine.

      Gets the set of output data types supported by the engine.

    • Field Detail

      • streamSource

        protected TwoStreamSource streamSource
        The TwoStreamSource object.

    • Constructor Detail

      • AbstractStreamSourceService

        public AbstractStreamSourceService()

    • Method Detail

      • configure

        public EngineData configure(EngineData input)
        Description copied from interface: Engine
        Configures the engine with the given input data.

        This method can be executed concurrently in several threads.

        Parameters:
        input - the data to configure the engine with
        Returns:
        an (optional) result and/or status of the configuration request, it could be null or without output data (but not recommended)

      • createStreamSource

        protected abstract TwoStreamSource createStreamSource(int port1,
                                                      int por2,
                                                      org.json.JSONObject opts)
                                               throws StreamSourceException
        Throws:
        StreamSourceException

      • closeStreamSource

        protected abstract void closeStreamSource()
        Closes the stream source.

      • readByteOrder

        protected abstract java.nio.ByteOrder readByteOrder()
                                             throws StreamSourceException
        Gets the byte order of the events in the stream.
        Returns:
        the byte order of the events in the stream.
        Throws:
        StreamSourceException - exception

      • getDataType

        protected abstract EngineDataType getDataType()
        Gets the ERSAP engine data-type for the type of the stream. The data-type will be used to serialize the events when the engine data result needs to be sent to services over the network.
        Returns:
        the data-type of the stream.

      • streamStart

        protected abstract void streamStart()

      • getStreamUnit

        protected abstract java.lang.Object getStreamUnit()
                                           throws StreamSourceException
        Throws:
        StreamSourceException

      • execute

        public EngineData execute(EngineData input)
        Description copied from interface: Engine
        Executes the engine with the given input data.

        This method can be executed concurrently in several threads.

        Parameters:
        input - the data to execute the engine with
        Returns:
        the result and/or status of the execution request, it cannot be null or without data (unless it is an error status), and it should not be the same input data reference

      • getInputDataTypes

        public java.util.Set<EngineDataType> getInputDataTypes()
        Description copied from interface: Engine
        Gets the set of input data types supported by the engine.
        Returns:
        the allowed input data types, it cannot be null nor empty

      • getOutputDataTypes

        public java.util.Set<EngineDataType> getOutputDataTypes()
        Description copied from interface: Engine
        Gets the set of output data types supported by the engine.
        Returns:
        the allowed output data types, it cannot be null nor empty
